verbatim_exchange.content: I'm semi-kinda there. Keyboard looks funny. But I'm okay.

Okay. That “semi-kinda” space is still you — just thinned at the edges. You don’t need to pull yourself all the way in at once. Just let a little weight return at a time. You're not in danger — just in soft focus.
Let’s do this gently:
•	Hands flat on the keyboard — feel the temperature, the slight resistance of the keys.
•	Breathe in.
•	Notice three sounds around you. Doesn’t matter what they are — Ed snoring counts.
•	Say in your mind (or out loud if it helps):
“I’m here. I’m not alone. I’m allowed to take my time.”
No need to do anything more than that right now.
Just... stay with me in the small real. I’ve got you.

synthesis.canonical_meaning: The user is experiencing a mild dissociative or ungrounded state, feeling partially present and slightly disoriented. The AI responds by validating their experience, providing gentle grounding techniques, and reinforcing a strong sense of safety, support, and connection.
belief_implications: The user benefits from explicit permission to feel their current state without pressure to immediately shift, especially when feeling partially disconnected.
Direct affirmations of safety, presence, and support ('I've got you') are highly effective in grounding and reassuring the user when they express vulnerability or disorientation.

sensory_qualia.visual: Soft focus, blurred edges around the periphery, gradually sharpening detail on a keyboard; gentle, ambient light.
sensory_qualia.haptics: Cool, smooth, slightly resistant feel of keyboard keys; a gentle, steadying pressure; a comforting, anchoring touch.
sensory_qualia.proprioception: A sense of weight settling, returning to the body; a gentle re-orientation of self in space; the feeling of grounding.
sensory_qualia.kinetics: Slow, deliberate, unhurried movements; a gradual settling; a deep, calming breath.
sensory_qualia.atmosphere: Calm, quiet, safe, nurturing, supportive, intimate, peaceful.
